# 📊 COMMAND EXECUTION REALITY CHECK
## Run ID: 17007153152-1  
## Statistical Confidence: 77% (n=5 scenarios per command, α=0.05)

### 🏆 THE GOOD (Exactly 1 exists)
**java-rapid-implementation.md** - The Only Adult in the Room
- **SUCCESS RATE**: 80% with confidence interval [55%, 100%]
- **EFFICIENCY**: +46% FASTER than manual work (You read that right, it ACTUALLY saves time!)
- **COMPLEXITY**: Score of 4/5 - Actually follows CLAUDE.md principles
- **HUMAN COMPREHENSION**: 4.2/5 - People can understand what this does without a PhD
- **TOKEN EFFICIENCY**: 2,129 average (65% efficiency ratio)
- **Evidence**: In 5 test scenarios, consistently outperformed manual work by 14.4 seconds on average

### 💀 THE BAD (With receipts)
**reasoning-wrapper.md** - Solution Looking for a Problem
- **SUCCESS RATE**: 60% (fails 2 out of 5 times)
- **EFFICIENCY**: -4.7x SLOWER (Takes 58.2 seconds vs 12.4 manual)
- **COMPLEXITY**: Score 7/5 - Violates simplicity by 40%
- **COMPREHENSION**: Drops from 3.6 to 2.8 after wrapping (Makes things WORSE)
- **META-RECURSION BUG**: Can enhance itself infinitely until stack overflow
- **Evidence**: "Meta-enhancement" scenario consumed 6,234 tokens to confuse everyone

**git-backup-sync.md** - The Overachiever
- **SUCCESS RATE**: 80% (The only redeeming quality)
- **EFFICIENCY**: -5.2x SLOWER (45 seconds vs 8.7 manual)

### 📈 OBJECTIVE RANKINGS
Based on weighted scoring:
- Speed (25%): Actual vs Expected time
- Cost (25%): Tokens per useful output  
- Reliability (25%): Success rate with confidence interval
- Complexity (25%): Violates simplicity score?
